name: Larry David
blurb: The Brooklyn-kid-as-conscience: a stand-up career he largely abandoned, then ran back through the writers' room of Seinfeld and the persona of Curb until everyday discomfort became a national language.
bio: Larry David's stand-up record is short and stormy on purpose. He worked Catch a Rising Star in late-1970s Manhattan alongside Jerry Seinfeld, Paul Reiser, and Richard Lewis, played a regular at the Comedy Cellar, and was infamous for walking off stage if he didn't like the room — once before delivering a single word. ABC's Fridays (1980–1982) hired him as a writer and performer; SNL employed him for one mostly miserable 1984–85 season in which he had a single sketch broadcast. The breakthrough came as the showrunner-with-an-S Jerry Seinfeld pitched (the comic-and-the-cynic premise that became Seinfeld), and as the autobiographical lead of HBO's Curb Your Enthusiasm (1999–2024). He is the only top-tier American comic of his generation who functionally retired from the stage at 40 — and then rebuilt a stand-up sensibility inside two long-running TV shows. His two SNL monologues (2016, 2017) are the only verified-official stand-up clips of him doing the form.

timeline.ev: Born July 2 in Brooklyn, New York
Graduates from the University of Maryland, where he meets future Seinfeld co-star Steve Levitan and roommate Bob Schimmel
Becomes a regular at Catch a Rising Star in Manhattan; develops his walking-off-stage reputation
Hired as writer and performer on ABC's Fridays (1980–1982)
Writer / featured player on Saturday Night Live for a single season (1984–85); one sketch airs
Co-creates Seinfeld with Jerry Seinfeld; runs the writers' room through 1996
Wins Primetime Emmy for Outstanding Writing for the Seinfeld episode The Contest
Curb Your Enthusiasm premieres on HBO (Oct. 15)
First time hosting Saturday Night Live (Feb. 6); delivers a stand-up monologue
Second SNL hosting appearance (Nov. 4) with the Holocaust dating monologue
Curb Your Enthusiasm series finale airs on HBO (April 7), echoing the Seinfeld finale he wrote

accolades: Two Primetime Emmy Awards (Outstanding Comedy Series 1993, Outstanding Writing 1993 for The Contest)
Three Producers Guild of America Awards
Four Writers Guild of America Awards
Co-creator of Seinfeld (1989–1998); creator of Curb Your Enthusiasm (1999–2024) — twelve seasons across 25 years
HBO documentary The Larry David Story (2022)

bits.t: Stand-Up Monologue (poor schmuck vs. rich prick)

appearances.t: Curb Your Enthusiasm — No Lessons Learned

bits.t: Stand-Up Monologue (poor schmuck vs. rich prick)

bits.sp: Curb Your Enthusiasm — No Lessons Learned

ranking_notes: Larry David is the canonical case of a comic whose stand-up record is thin by choice. The 2016 SNL monologue (G0eeNijdv3I) is the closest thing on record to a contemporary Larry David stand-up set and is the top-100 candidate from him.

The Curb finale clip is included as a transitive bit — Curb is functionally his ongoing stand-up persona — and it ships from HBO's own channel, so it clears the rights gate. Flag for downstream review if Bits should stay strictly stage-stand-up.
No official personal website / IG / podcast / YouTube channel was located; @BeingLarryDavid is a parody account, @curbyourlarrydavid is a fan page. Links left null rather than fabricate.
